stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 4 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 10 observations with mean 79.4 % (within-trial site SD = 5.8). trial site B has 15 observations with mean 47.6 % (within-trial site SD = 7.9). trial site C has 5 observations with mean 58.2 % (within-trial site SD = 4.9). trial site D has 20 observations with mean 90.0 % (within-trial site SD = 9.7). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 8.8 and 21.9 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site D: 89.6 % (shrunk from raw 90.0). Range [89.0, 89.8] as between-trial site SD varies from 8.8 to 21.9. Pooled mean: 72.0.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 72.0 % (weighted average of 4 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=14.6: B = 0.978.
Interval [89.0, 89.8] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (90.0) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (72.0) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
